questions.question: What is the main characteristic of a plain function?
options: It has a domain and range
questions.explanation: A plain function is a relation where each input corresponds to exactly one output.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a one-to-one function?
questions.explanation: A function is one-to-one if each output value corresponds to exactly one input value.

questions.question: What does it mean for a function to be onto?
options: Each input corresponds to exactly one output
Each output corresponds to at least one input
questions.explanation: A function is onto if each output value has at least one input value.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a plain function but not a one-to-one function?
options: It has a domain and range
questions.explanation: A plain function is a relation where each input corresponds to exactly one output, but it may not be one-to-one.

questions.question: What is the primary difference between a plain function and a one-to-one function?
options: A one-to-one function is a relation where each output corresponds to exactly one input
A plain function is a relation where each input corresponds to exactly one output
A one-to-one function is a function where each output corresponds to at least one input
A plain function is a function where each input corresponds to at least one output
questions.explanation: The primary difference between a plain function and a one-to-one function is that a one-to-one function requires each output value to correspond to exactly one input value, while a plain function only requires each input to correspond to exactly one output value.
